# criblio_secret (Resource)

Secret Resource

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "criblio_secret" "my_secret" {
  description = "Database credentials"
  group_id = "default"
  id = "db-credentials"
  password = "db_password"
  secret_type = "credentials"
  username = "db_user"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tools/codegen -->
## Schema

### Required

- `group_id` (String) Worker group ID.
- `id` (String) Unique identifier for the secret.
- `secret_type` (String)

### Optional

- `api_key` (String, Sensitive) API key or access key. Required when <code>secretType</code> is <code>keypair</code>.
- `description` (String) Brief description of the secret.
- `password` (String, Sensitive) Password. Required when <code>secretType</code> is <code>credentials</code>.
- `secret_key` (String, Sensitive) Secret key. Required when <code>secretType</code> is <code>keypair</code>.
- `tags` (String) Tags for categorizing or organizing the secret.
- `username` (String) Username. Required when <code>secretType</code> is <code>credentials</code>.
- `value` (String, Sensitive) Secret value. Required when <code>secretType</code> is <code>text</code>.

### Read-Only

- `folder_id` (String) Folder ID for a shared global secret. Present only when the row originates from a global secret folder.
- `read_only` (Boolean) If <code>true</code>, the secret is read-only because it is defined in global scope.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

In Terraform v1.5.0 and later, the [`import` block](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/import) can be used with the `id` attribute, for example:

```terraform
import {
  to = criblio_secret.my_criblio_secret
  id = jsonencode({
    group_id = "default"
    id       = "secret-s3"
  })
}
```

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
terraform import criblio_secret.my_criblio_secret '{"group_id": "default", "id": "secret-s3"}'
```
